_purpose: Orchestrator continuity across Claude Code sessions. The HUMAN talks to ONE AI (the orchestrator); that AI dispatches scope-specific work via the Agent tool. When the human starts a fresh Claude Code session, the new orchestrator MUST read this file early in startup so it knows: what was last being worked on, what's pending, what decisions were just made, what scopes are currently claimed, what to NOT re-do.
_protocol: Update this file at every significant decision/checkpoint. Treat it like a running session journal — newest at the bottom of recent_decisions, oldest pruned. The aggregator script `node scripts/agent-status.js` complements this with per-scope status from data/coordination/sessions/*.json.

NEXT ARC: Phase 3 (P2P transport, relay = signaling-only) → Phase 4 (relay-independence — the payoff: a group with ≥1 reachable peer survives a dead home relay) → Phase 5 (mDNS/DHT discovery). Other queued polish: native right-click context menu parity; creator-auto-rekey-from-list.
open_questions_for_human: Cleanup orphan Ed25519 admin rows: verified 2026-05-20 that 4 pre-PQ Ed25519 admin pubkeys (64-char hex) are still in user_roles. They come from the ADMIN_KEYS env var which the relay startup re-INSERTs on every boot. They can't log in (Inc3b gates them) but they're confusion-bait. Operator should: update ADMIN_KEYS in /opt/Humanity/.env to contain the operator's current Dilithium hex (Shaostoul's key in registered_names), DELETE FROM user_roles WHERE LENGTH(public_key) = 64, systemctl restart humanity-relay, verify only Dilithium-length pubkeys remain.

recent_decisions.decision: PLAY-TEST FIXES v0.343.0 (operator's FIRST live play of the gameplay arc). ROOT CAUSE of most reported symptoms = a STALE data dir shadowing the live repo data: find_data_dir preferred a 'full' data/ beside the exe over the repo's own, and extract_data_if_needed writes embedded data beside the build exe ONCE and never refreshes — so a prior run's target/release/data (frozen, missing getting_started.ron + pre-remap recipes.csv) was used when running target/release/HumanityOS.exe DIRECTLY. That made: the quest show its raw id 'gs_first_steps' with no steps (registry lacked the def), Dev:stock no-op (empty recipe inputs), skills stuck (registries stale/empty). 'just launch'/'just play' run the ARCHIVED exe in C:\Humanity\ which DOES use C:\Humanity\data, so the bug only hit direct target/release runs. FIXES: (1) find_data_dir now PREFERS a repo data dir (parent contains Cargo.toml) over an extracted copy -> dev runs from any exe location read LIVE data; (2) deleted the stale target/release/data; (3) added getting_started.ron to the embedded-extraction list (+ flagged that the hand-list has drifted: status_effects.csv/containers/food_system.ron are loaded but not extracted — tracked for distributed builds). UX feedback also addressed: (4) the Mining panel shows each drone's 3-STAGE trip (Stage 1/3 outbound -> 2/3 mining -> 3/3 returning home) with a per-stage PROGRESS BAR (new GuiDrone.phase_progress from mining::phase_secs) — the 'show the ship is out' cue the operator asked for; (5) commission buttons relabeled '{ore} . {N} left' + header 'hauls up to 10 per trip' (the big number was misread as the per-trip haul); (6) the Skills panel now shows the REAL skill sheet (every registry skill + live level/XP, grouped by category) instead of the old misleading static placeholders (Building/Trading/etc. at %). VERIFIED 385/385 native lib + relay clean. Drink becomes testable too (Dev:stock yields water_bottle, a recipe input = a drinkable). LESSON: the extracted-beside-exe data dir is a recurring FOOTGUN (frozen on first run) — the Cargo.toml-sibling repo-preference fixes dev; the extraction list should later be derived from embedded_data (single source of truth) for distributed builds. SEPARATE GAP (logged, not fixed): the 3D player home is empty (no placed crafting stations / interactables — the loops are menu-driven, not yet walk-up-to-a-station in the world); that's a world-content arc.
recent_decisions.why: First live play = the test-and-harden the build order culminated in. Most 'bugs' traced to ONE root cause (stale data dir), so fixing data resolution unblocked dev-stock + quests + skills + drink at once — far higher leverage than patching each symptom. Preferred the repo data via a Cargo.toml-sibling heuristic (cleanly distinguishes dev tree from a distributed install) rather than deleting/disabling extraction (distributed builds still need it). Bundled the UX asks (drone stages, labels, real skill sheet) into the same pass because they're exactly what the operator was inspecting. The home-empty world-content gap + the extraction-list drift are real but bigger/lower-priority (no users yet) — tracked, not force-fixed, to keep the fix increment tight and get the operator unblocked fast.

recent_decisions.decision: PROGRESSION #8c — QUESTS SHIPPED v0.342.0 — completes the Progression layer (#8) AND the whole gameplay-loop arc (#1-#8). The quests/ scaffold (QuestDef/QuestRegistry/ActiveQuest/QuestTracker/QuestSystem + objectives enum + data/quests/*.ron) was built-but-unwired. Wired: QuestRegistry::from_ron_dir reads every data/quests/*.ron (each a Vec<QuestDef> via ron::from_str) into DataStore quest_registry; QuestSystem registered LAST; the player spawns with a QuestTracker auto-accepting 'gs_first_steps'. NEW data/quests/getting_started.ron = a SATISFIABLE Gather/Craft chain (the existing tutorial/farming/construction chains use Build objectives that need the still-deferred ConstructionSystem, so they load but can't complete — a fresh player needs a finishable quest). Count-based objectives (Craft/Harvest) advance via a NEW shared 'quest_events' channel (Mutex<Vec<String>>) the action systems push to on completion (CraftingSystem::on_craft_complete -> 'craft_<recipe>'; FarmingSystem harvest -> 'harvest_<crop>'; the SAME completion hooks as the #8a XP awards); QuestSystem.tick drains the channel + increments each active quest's progress[key], then checks objectives; Gather objectives read live inventory directly (no events). Completion grants item rewards + AUTO-ACCEPTS dependents (prerequisite chaining: finishing gs_first_steps auto-accepts gs_toolsmith). GUI: a new profile ProfileSection::Quests panel shows active quests (Step X/Y + description + progress bar) + completed (checkmark). QuestSystem removed from engine_wiring_lint DEFERRED + quest_registry added to REQUIRED_RUNTIME_DATA (12 systems now tick). BONUS FIX (#8b fresh-player DEADLOCK): a new player is level 0 in every skill and the ONLY metalworking-XP source is crafting metalworking recipes, so gating level-1 recipes made every skill un-bootstrappable (only Dev:max-skills escaped it) — fixed by making skill_level 0/1 the free STARTER TIER (meets_skill_requirement + the GUI gate only block skill_level >= 2); the existing skill-gate test uses level 3 so it still passes. VERIFIED: 385/385 native lib (3 new quest tests: RON-dir load, Gather->complete+reward, craft-event->complete->prereq-chain) + relay cargo check clean + native check clean. ★ GAMEPLAY-LOOP ARC COMPLETE: build order #1-#8 all shipped v0.329->v0.342 (21 commits) = the full production + survival + progression sandbox. NEXT is a real phase boundary (the approved build order is done) -> surface the next-arc choice to the operator.
recent_decisions.why: #8c is the last build-order item; quests give the free sandbox a guided GOAL structure (the 'what do I do next' layer). Wired the existing scaffold (it was sound) rather than rebuild. The quest_events channel mirrors the #8a xp_grants decoupling (action systems hold &DataStore, not &mut QuestSystem) + reuses the exact same craft/harvest completion hooks = minimal new surface. Authored a SATISFIABLE starter chain because the existing quest data is Build-heavy (ConstructionSystem deferred) and thus uncompletable — a fresh player must have a quest they can actually finish, or the feature is hollow. Caught + fixed the #8b deadlock while designing the chain (a fresh player literally couldn't smelt iron to progress) — the 'correctness via careful work' the operator wants; 'level-1 free, level-2+ gated' is the standard basic-free/mastery-gated design. Native QuestSystem drives single-player; the relay's separate quest chain is for MMO — reconciliation deferred (#8c-tail) since single-player is the testable mode now. The arc being COMPLETE is a genuine direction fork (the approved plan is done; the next arc — test/harden vs multiplayer vs content vs other — is the operator's call), so surface it rather than unilaterally start a new multi-increment arc.

recent_decisions.decision: PROGRESSION #8b — TECH-UNLOCK SHIPPED v0.341.0 — skills now GATE crafting (the payoff that makes #8a's XP matter). CraftingSystem::meets_skill_requirement rejects a craft when the crafter's PlayerSkills.level(recipe.skill_required) < recipe.skill_level, checked in the request loop BEFORE consuming inputs (so a blocked craft consumes nothing). AUTHORITATIVE: enforced system-side, not just greyed in the GUI -- a bot/non-GUI craft path can't bypass it. An entity WITHOUT a PlayerSkills component (e.g. an NPC/bot crafter) is NOT blocked (the gate only constrains skilled actors = the player). GUI: GuiRecipe + load_crafting_recipes now parse skill_required/skill_level (were dropped); the crafting page shows 'Requires {skill} Lv N (you: Lv M)' per recipe (text_muted when met, danger when not) + locks the Craft button with a distinct 'Skill level too low' vs 'Missing ingredients' message; skill display name resolves via the live GuiState.skills (fallback = capitalised id). A 'Dev: max skills' button (profile Skills panel) sets pending_dev_max_skills -> dev_max_skills bool channel -> SkillSystem maxes EVERY registered skill to its max_level on the player (mirrors Dev:stock-materials / Dev:grow-crops; drained before the XP early-return so it runs with no pending XP). PROVEN by crafting::skill_xp_tests::skill_gate_blocks_under_level_then_allows (Lv1<3 blocked, ore intact; raise to Lv3 -> crafts, consumes ore) + skills::skill_tests::dev_max_skills_maxes_every_skill (mining->25, foraging->20, all present). VERIFIED: 382/382 native lib + relay cargo check clean + native check clean. No engine_wiring_lint change (extended CraftingSystem + SkillSystem; no new System registered). #8c quests NEXT (wire the built-but-unwired quests/ scaffold; reconcile native-vs-relay quest ownership).
recent_decisions.why: #8b is the PAYOFF of #8a: skills earning XP is hollow unless they GATE or buff something, and gating crafting is the most direct 'your level matters.' Enforced AUTHORITATIVELY in CraftingSystem (not just a greyed GUI button) so a bot/non-GUI path can't craft above its level -- the same fail-closed posture as the rest of the platform. The Dev:max-skills affordance was MANDATORY to preserve the operator's 'develop as if 100% unlocked' testing posture: once crafting is skill-gated, high-level recipes become untestable without grinding, so a one-click max (mirroring the existing Dev:stock-materials / Dev:grow-crops affordances) keeps every loop testable. NPCs/bots (no PlayerSkills) are intentionally not gated so the check doesn't break non-player crafters. Showed the requirement + current level on each recipe so the player knows WHAT to level (not just a dead button).

recent_decisions.decision: PROGRESSION #8a — SKILLS + XP FOUNDATION SHIPPED v0.340.0 (operator chose 'Progression (#8)' via AskUserQuestion at the sandbox-complete phase boundary). The skills/ scaffold (SkillDef/SkillProgress/PlayerSkills/SkillRegistry/SkillSystem, exponential xp_base*level^1.5 curve) was ~90% built but DEAD (registry never loaded, system never registered, no XP sources, PlayerSkills never spawned). Wired end-to-end (scoped by a read-only Explore agent mapping skills/quests infra, VERIFIED before building): added SkillRegistry::from_csv (parses data/skills/skills.csv -> 20 skills; SkillDef fields map 1:1 to columns so parse_csv deserializes direct); load it into DataStore['skill_registry']; spawn PlayerSkills on the player; register SkillSystem LAST (after the action systems) so it drains a NEW shared 'xp_grants' DataStore channel (Mutex<Vec<SkillXPEvent>>) the action systems push to via a shared award_skill_xp(data,skill,amt) helper -- the decoupled path since they hold &DataStore not &mut SkillSystem. XP SOURCES wired: craft completion -> recipe.skill_required (10 + skill_level*5), harvest -> farming (10+qty*2), mine-deliver -> mining (qty). Live levels+XP render in the EXISTING profile Skills panel (was static defaults from default_profile.json; now shows live when gui_state.skills non-empty). DATA-INTEGRITY FIX (no-bandaid, the core of this increment): recipes.csv skill_required used a NON-CANONICAL vocabulary (smithing/textile/medical/chemistry/crafting/construction) matching NO skill in skills.csv (canonical: metalworking/tailoring/medicine/alchemy/woodworking/shelter_building per schemas/skill.toml) -> every craft XP award would have SILENTLY no-op'd on the registry lookup miss (the exact silent-nothing the engine_wiring_lint exists to kill). Reconciled all 235 rows via a category-aware node remap (smithing->metalworking, textile->tailoring, medical->medicine, chemistry->alchemy, construction->shelter_building; the 'crafting' grab-bag by recipe category: textile->tailoring, electronics/assembly->engineering, chemistry->medicine, else->woodworking; electronics->engineering) -> every recipe skill now resolves. The Recipe struct + RecipeRow + from_csv now PARSE skill_required+skill_level (were dropped/ignored). NEW unit drift-lint skills::skill_tests::every_recipe_skill_is_a_real_skill makes the two vocabularies impossible to drift apart again (theme_token_lint philosophy). SkillSystem REMOVED from engine_wiring_lint DEFERRED + 'skill_registry' added to REQUIRED_RUNTIME_DATA. Registered via the SHORT form register(SkillSystem::new()) (imported the skills types) because the lint reads the ident after 'register(' -- a fully-qualified path would read 'crate' and the lint would not see it registered. VERIFIED: 380/380 native lib (4 new: registry parse, channel->level-up, the drift lint, end-to-end craft->XP) + relay cargo check clean + native check clean. #8b tech-unlock (enforce skill_level in can_craft -> skills GATE content) + #8c quests (wire the built-but-unwired quests/ scaffold; reconcile native-vs-relay quest ownership) NEXT.
recent_decisions.why: Operator picked Progression (#8) at the phase boundary (16-commit sandbox complete). Skills+XP is the FOUNDATION (quests + tech-unlock build on it), so it goes first. The vocabulary mismatch was a LATENT silent-no-op bug: granting XP to 'smithing' when the skill id is 'metalworking' returns None -> XP vanishes with no error -- exactly the class the lints exist to prevent, so fixing it holistically + adding a drift lint (not a one-off patch) is mandatory per the no-bandaids rule. Honored the EXISTING design (schemas/skill.toml's canonical 20-skill vocabulary + its 'don't add a generic crafting skill / maintain category balance' guidance) -> reconciled recipes.csv TO the schema, not the reverse. WIRED the existing scaffold rather than rebuild (it was sound). SkillSystem drains a DataStore channel because action systems can't hold &mut SkillSystem; registered LAST so grants pushed earlier the same frame apply that frame (before the ECS->GUI sync reads them). Rendered into the existing profile Skills section rather than a new panel/page -> no inventory-page bloat, uses the skills' natural home. The 'crafting' grab-bag remap is category-coarse (a clock -> woodworking) -- a documented tuning item, not silent debt; the MECHANISM is correct + complete.

recent_decisions.decision: DRINK ACTION SHIPPED v0.339.0 (#3b-tail) — completes hydration symmetry (you could Eat but not drink; thirst was only fixable via watery food). Drink items (subcategory 'drink'/'liquid' or water_* prefix: water_bottle/water_purified/juice/coffee/tea/milk/energy_drink) get a 'Drink' button in the inventory detail panel (checked BEFORE Eat, since drinks are category 'food' too) -> drink_request channel -> FoodSystem consumes 1 + hydration += 30 (mirrors the eat handler; query (Inventory,Vitals), first=player). PROVEN by food::drinking_restores_hydration. VERIFIED: 376/376 native lib + relay clean. No engine_wiring_lint change. Small bounded low-risk increment (mirrors Eat). Remaining #3b-tail: stamina/vision (renderer) modifiers, spoilage->nutrition.
recent_decisions.why: After all 5 survival needs, the obvious gap: Eat existed but Drink didn't (thirst rose but was only fixable via watery food = asymmetric/odd). Drink mirrors Eat exactly (consume item -> hydration), low-risk, completes the hydration loop. Drinks are category 'food' subcategory 'drink', so the GUI checks is_drink BEFORE is_food (else Eat would show + no-op on a beverage that has no nutrition profile).

recent_decisions.decision: GAMEPLAY-LOOP ARC #7c-sanitation SHIPPED v0.338.0 — the COMPOST loop (operator picked 'Sanitation -> compost loop' via AskUserQuestion). Closes the food -> waste -> compost -> soil -> food cycle. New Vitals.waste/waste_max (serde-default). FoodSystem: waste ACCRUES (0.05/s living + 4/meal); above 75% applies a new 'unsanitary' debuff (speed:0.95, status_effects.csv); a COMPOST action (compost_request bool channel) converts the player's waste -> fertilizer_0 items (waste/25 floored) + clears waste (reuses the existing fertilizer_0 item + an item_registry max_stack lookup). FarmingSystem: a FERTILIZE action (fertilize_crop_request entity-bits channel) consumes 1 fertilizer_0 -> +40 crop health (growth is health-weighted -> speeds growth) -> closes the loop back to gardening (#4). GUI: inventory vitals panel gains a Waste bar (inverted color, high=bad) + a Compost button; the Garden panel crops gain a Fertilize button (beside Water/Harvest). PROVEN by food::waste_accrues_unsanitary_and_composts_to_fertilizer (waste 80 -> unsanitary; compost -> >=3 fertilizer + waste cleared + unsanitary lifts) + farming::fertilize_consumes_fertilizer_and_boosts_crop_health. VERIFIED: 375/375 native lib + relay cargo check clean. No engine_wiring_lint change (extended FoodSystem + FarmingSystem; no new System). The compost query is (Inventory,Vitals)-first (consistent with the eat pass; dropped a Controllable filter after a test surfaced the player-without-Controllable case). ALL FIVE listed survival needs now LIVE: nutrition (satiation/hydration), energy/rest, oxygen, temperature, sanitation. REMAINING #7: register atmosphere/hydrology/ecology/disasters WHEN they have real consumers (not cosmetic un-deferral, the weather precedent); #8 progression LAST.
recent_decisions.why: Operator chose sanitation -> compost. Framed as REAL composting (organic waste -> fertilizer -> soil) = a genuine survival skill (the project's mission) + it closes a CIRCULAR loop (waste isn't wasted; it feeds the crops that feed you). Reused the existing fertilizer_0 item + the established channel / Vitals / crop-action patterns (a 4th survival action area). Kept the compost query consistent with the eat pass (Inventory+Vitals, first match = player; no Controllable filter) after a test surfaced the player-without-Controllable case. This completes the survival-needs list the operator approved; the remaining #7 is registering the heavier sim systems WHEN they have real consumers, not cosmetically.

recent_decisions.decision: GAMEPLAY-LOOP ARC #7c SHIPPED v0.337.0 — registered WeatherSystem (10 ECS systems now tick) + tied weather into survival. WeatherSystem was COMPLETE (7 conditions, season-driven temperature Spring 15 / Summer 30 / Autumn 12 / Winter -2 + condition modifiers, smooth transitions) but DEAD: never registered, never exported. Now: WeatherSystem.tick exports self.weather to a Mutex<Weather> in the DataStore (the TimeSystem/game_time pattern); registered right after TimeSystem so it reads the exported season. The main-loop env-context computation reads the weather temperature as the EXPOSED ambient_temp_c (was a fixed -40) -> winter/storms make the outside deadlier (faster hypothermia). The pre-existing GUI weather bridge (it read get::Weather but nothing wrote it) was fixed to read the Mutex -> the weather HUD now works. Removed WeatherSystem from engine_wiring_lint DEFERRED. PROVEN by weather::exports_weather_to_datastore (after a forced transition + ticks the DataStore weather matches the system's internal state). VERIFIED: 373/373 native lib + relay cargo check clean + WeatherSystem registered and not-deferred. This is the FIRST deferred SIM system off the lint list with a REAL consumer (survival temperature), not a cosmetic registration. ABSTRACTION NOTE: 'outside the sealed homestead = hostile (no air, #7b) + weather-affected temperature (#7c)' merges the space-vacuum model with planet weather -- a playable abstraction that sidesteps the space-vs-planet worldbuilding question (the operator's call; logged #7b-tail). REMAINING #7: sanitation/waste; register atmosphere/hydrology/ecology/disasters as their content spawns.
recent_decisions.why: Build order #7 includes 'register the sim systems.' WeatherSystem was the cleanest to register WITH a real consumer (already complete; only needed export + a reader): the survival exposed-temperature is that reader, so winter/storms now matter -> deeper survival AND a deferred system goes live = two #7 goals at once. Reused the TimeSystem game_time export (Mutex) pattern + fixed the pre-existing-but-dead GUI weather bridge in the same stroke. Merged weather-temp with the no-oxygen-outside model as a playable abstraction rather than forcing a space-vs-planet worldbuilding decision (that's the operator's; flagged #7b-tail). Registering a sim system only when it has a real consumer keeps the lint honest (not cosmetic un-deferral).

recent_decisions.decision: GAMEPLAY-LOOP ARC #7b SHIPPED v0.336.0 — environment-coupled oxygen + temperature (operator chose 'build the layer' over maintained-supply meters via AskUserQuestion). Scoped via a read-only Explore agent (mapped ship/room/atmosphere infra: RoomInfo{center,dimensions} AABBs in ship/fibonacci.rs, camera.position = player pos, RoomEnvironment/Atmosphere/EnclosedSpace exist but unused; it recommended point-in-bounds). Built it (VERIFYING the map, not trusting): EngineState now stores a homestead_bounds AABB encompassing ALL rooms (a single envelope, computed at homestead-gen from room_info, so corridors/gaps don't falsely read as exposed). The main loop computes an EnvironmentContext{sealed,oxygenated,ambient_temp_c} each frame from camera.position vs that AABB (inside = sealed/oxygenated/21C; outside = vacuum/-40C) -> DataStore. New Vitals fields oxygen + body_temp_c (serde-default for old saves). FoodSystem's decay pass reads the context, and was REFACTORED to accumulate health_drain across ALL sources (Option<&mut Health> moves once): oxygen recovers when oxygenated / drains 2.5/s in vacuum -> hypoxia (<50) then suffocation (0, +8hp/s); body_temp drifts toward 37 sealed / ambient exposed at 0.5C/s -> hypothermia (<35) / heat_exhaustion (>39) +2hp/s. New status effects hypoxia/suffocation/heat_exhaustion (speed debuffs, tangible via #3b) + REUSED the pre-existing hypothermia (removed a duplicate my CSV splice had added). ALSO made HUNGER tangible: hungry stamina_regen:0.7 (inert, no stamina system) -> speed:0.9, consistent with thirst/fatigue (the last inert deficiency). GUI: inventory vitals panel gains an oxygen bar + body-temp readout + a Sealed/EXPOSED status line. PROVEN by food::exposure_drains_oxygen_and_chills_then_recovers_when_sealed (exposed 30 ticks -> oxygen<50 + body<35 + hypoxia + hypothermia + health<100; re-seal 15 ticks -> oxygen>50 + oxygen conditions clear). VERIFIED: 372/372 native lib + relay cargo check clean + degree-sign glyph confirmed safe (Latin-1, not in icon_glyph_lint BROKEN_GLYPHS). No engine_wiring_lint change (extended FoodSystem). Walking outside the homestead is now a real threat = the space/asteroid-survival groundwork. #7c NEXT: sanitation/waste, then register the heavier sim systems (weather/atmosphere/hydrology/disasters) as content spawns. #7b-tail: real per-zone enclosed-space/airlock volumes + suit/clothing insulation (vs the single homestead AABB); tie body-temp into RoomEnvironment/HVAC (both exist, unused).
recent_decisions.why: Operator picked environment-coupled (not maintained-supply meters) -> the bigger but 'do it right' path that makes LOCATION matter + sets up space/asteroid gameplay. Used a single encompassing homestead AABB (not per-room) for the MVP because per-room bounds would falsely flag corridors/gaps as 'exposed' (oxygen draining in your own hallway = annoying); the envelope models inside-the-base vs outside-the-hull, a clean movement-driven loop. Refactored the decay pass to accumulate health_drain (one Option<&mut Health> consume) once a 3rd/4th health source (suffocation, temp) joined starvation/dehydration. Folded in the hunger-tangibility fix (1-line data) since it was the same file + completes the operator's 'deficiency -> debuff' vision (hunger was the last inert one). Verified the Explore map (point-in-bounds, camera pos) BEFORE building (verify-don't-trust).

recent_decisions.decision: GAMEPLAY-LOOP ARC #7a SHIPPED v0.335.0 — survival systems begin: ENERGY/REST. Operator returned + said 'keep on going. The game isn't crashing' (confirms they were away during the 7-ship run + the loops run clean). Added energy + energy_max to the Vitals component (serde(default)=100 so pre-energy saves still load); FoodSystem (the de-facto vitals manager) decays energy while awake (0.06/s, full->fatigued ~20min), applies a new 'fatigued' condition (status_effects.csv: speed:0.85:multiply) below 25% energy -- TANGIBLE via the #3b camera speed_multiplier (fatigued -> -15% move speed), cleared on recovery. A Rest button (inventory vitals panel) writes a rest_request channel -> FoodSystem refills energy to max + clears fatigued + grants the existing 'rested' buff. Survival baseline is now satiation + hydration + ENERGY (all the #3 vitals pattern). GUI: an Energy bar + Rest button beside the satiation/hydration bars. Rest is INSTANT (no time cost) for now -- a real sleep time-skip needs a TimeSystem time-skip channel (TimeSystem OWNS + overwrites game_time each tick from its internal elapsed_seconds, so FoodSystem can't advance the clock directly) = tracked #7a-tail. PROVEN by food::low_energy_fatigues_and_rest_restores (energy 10 -> fatigued applied; rest_request -> energy >90 + fatigued cleared). VERIFIED: 371/371 native lib + relay cargo check clean. No engine_wiring_lint change (extended the already-registered FoodSystem; no new System). #7b NEXT (tracked): oxygen + temperature -- ENVIRONMENT-COUPLED (a real loop needs an enclosed-space/shelter/ambient layer, not always-on meters); then sanitation/waste; then register the heavier sim systems (weather/atmosphere/hydrology/disasters) as their content spawns.
recent_decisions.why: Operator said 'keep on going' -> resumed the build order at #7. Picked ENERGY/REST first because it's the only survival need that's fully SELF-CONTAINED (no environment-context layer needed -- you tire over time, you rest), so it ships as a real testable loop NOW, vs oxygen/temperature which need a vacuum/shelter/ambient signal that doesn't exist yet (#7b). Made it tangible by reusing the #3b speed mechanic (fatigued = a speed debuff) rather than the inert stamina_regen path, so low energy actually slows you. Extended FoodSystem (already the vitals manager) instead of a new SurvivalSystem to avoid churn + cross-system effect-ticking fragility (one system owns the full Vitals+effects lifecycle); noted the naming wart. Kept rest instant because a clean time-skip needs a TimeSystem change -- logged, not faked.

recent_decisions.decision: GAMEPLAY-LOOP ARC #3b SHIPPED v0.334.0 — buffs/debuffs now mechanically AFFECT movement (the operator's explicit 'increased move speed' vision, previously visible-but-inert). #3's nutrition loop applied well_fed/hungry/thirsty/food_poisoning but NO system consumed their stat:value:op modifiers -> a half-feature (chips shown, zero gameplay effect). Now: CameraController gains a speed_multiplier field (default 1.0; applied to first-person + vertical movement, look/rotation untouched); the main loop computes it each frame from the player's active StatusEffects via the new StatusEffectRegistry::net_stat_multiplier(ids, 'speed') (product of multiply mods, sum of add mods, clamped >= 0) and sets it before update_camera. Added a new well_nourished buff (status_effects.csv: speed:1.1:multiply, 1800s) that FoodSystem applies when satiation >= WELL_FED_THRESHOLD (alongside well_fed) -> a good meal = +10% move speed (the upside). Existing debuffs are tangible too: thirsty/flu (speed:0.8) -> -20%. PROVEN by status_effects::net_speed_multiplier_combines_effects (empty -> 1.0; thirsty -> 0.8; well_nourished+thirsty -> 0.88 multiplicative; well_fed [stamina_regen, NOT speed] -> 1.0 = correct stat isolation). VERIFIED: 370/370 native lib + relay cargo check clean. No engine_wiring_lint change (no new System; the multiplier is main-loop-computed from the registry). STILL DEFERRED (#3b-tail, tracked): stamina_regen mods (well_fed/hungry/exhausted) need a stamina system to matter; vision_range/night_vision needs renderer wiring; reload-speed needs a combat hook. Picked SPEED first = the operator's literal example + the cleanest contained hook (confirmed the movement reality: the ECS PlayerControllerSystem NO-OPS today -- no PhysicsBody player + no physics_world in DataStore -> movement is camera-driven, so camera.speed is where movement ACTUALLY happens).
recent_decisions.why: The operator explicitly described buffs ('increased move/reload/improved night vision'). #3 shipped the effects but they were INERT -- a visible-chips-no-effect half-feature = exactly the bandaid the no-bandaids directive forbids. #3b closes it for SPEED (his first example) via the cleanest contained hook: a camera speed_multiplier driven by the registry's modifier parse. Added well_nourished so the UPSIDE (good food -> faster) lands, not just debuffs. Kept stamina_regen/vision deferred because they need NEW subsystems (stamina, renderer vision) -- logged, not faked. Verified the movement path is camera-driven (ECS-physics dead) BEFORE wiring, so the hook is where movement really happens, not the dead PlayerControllerSystem -- the Explore-agent-style 'check the code, don't assume' discipline.

recent_decisions.decision: GAMEPLAY-LOOP ARC #6 SHIPPED v0.333.0 — refining-chain depth (closes mine -> refine -> craft). #5's drones mine nickel/platinum ore but nothing could refine it -> the chain dead-ended at raw ore. Added 3 items (nickel_ingot_0, platinum_ingot_0, stainless_steel_ingot_0) + 3 recipes to data/items.csv + data/recipes.csv: smelt_nickel (nickel_ore_0:2|coal_0:1 -> nickel_ingot_0), smelt_platinum (platinum_ore_0:2|coal_0:2 -> platinum_ingot_0), smelt_stainless (iron_ingot_0:2|nickel_ingot_0:1|coal_0:1 -> stainless_steel_ingot_0). 2-TIER depth: ore -> ingot -> alloy (smelt_stainless consumes nickel_ingot_0, itself smelt_nickel's output -> not a flat one-step tree). Inserted EOL-aware via a node splice (the CSVs are CRLF with a pre-existing mid-line carriage-return quirk; a minimal-diff slice insertion, NOT a full rejoin that would normalize + noise the whole file). PURE DATA + one test — no code/system change (the #2 crafting mechanism already executes ANY recipe; refining IS smelting recipes). LOCKED by crafting::refining_chain_tests (the 3 recipes exist, every input/output is a real items.csv id -> catches a typo the CSV parser would silently skip, and stainless's nickel_ingot_0 input proves the multi-tier dependency). VERIFIED: 369/369 native lib + relay cargo check clean. #6b DEFERRED (tracked in gameplay-loops.md): deeper trees (platinum -> catalysts/electronics, more alloys), recipe BYPRODUCTS (schema supports, data missing), chemistry -> crafting links — the broad DB expansion the 2026-05-29 audit flagged, now meaningfully consumable because crafting + refining actually RUN.
recent_decisions.why: Build order #6. #5 made drones deliver nickel/platinum ore but nothing refined it, so the mine->process chain dead-ended at raw ore. Closed it with the smallest correct change: 3 ingot items + 3 smelt recipes (refining = smelting, already executable by the crafting system). Added a test because a typo'd output item id parses fine yet silently produces a nonexistent item -> the test asserts every input/output is real + the multi-tier dependency, locking the data the way theme_token_lint locks colors. Held the broad chemistry/byproduct DB expansion as #6b: it's finally worth doing (real consumers exist now) but it's breadth, not the core loop.

recent_decisions.decision: GAMEPLAY-LOOP ARC #5 SHIPPED v0.332.0 — drone->asteroid mining (the operator's core acquisition mechanic). New ECS components: AsteroidBody{name, classification, ores:Vec<(item_id,f32)>} = a FINITE multi-ore body (with take()/total_remaining()/has_ore()) + Drone{home, target, ore_id, phase, phase_time, cargo} + DronePhase{Outbound,Mining,Returning,Done}. New REGISTERED DroneSystem (9 systems now tick). Reuses the GUI->ECS bridge (4th consumer): a commission_drone channel the Mining panel writes an ore id to. DroneSystem.tick: (1) COMMISSION -> find the player (home) + an asteroid that has the ore, spawn a Drone (Outbound); (2) ADVANCE -> a query_mut<&mut Drone> loop advances phase_time + transitions phases, recording cross-entity INTENTS (Mine{drone,asteroid,ore} on Outbound->Mining; Deliver{drone,home,ore,qty} on Returning->Done) so the &mut World mutations run AFTER the drone query releases; (3) APPLY -> Mine takes up to DRONE_CAPACITY(10) from the asteroid's AsteroidBody into the drone cargo, Deliver adds cargo to the home inventory + despawns the drone; (4) DELETE asteroids whose total_remaining < 1 (the operator's 'consumed -> deleted'). Added nickel_ore_0 + platinum_ore_0 to items.csv (matched the file's messy CRLF + embedded-\r format via a node splice). lib.rs spawns 2 test asteroids (M-12: iron/nickel/platinum; S-7: iron/copper). GUI: a Mining panel on the inventory page lists asteroids + remaining ore, a 'Commission drone' button per distinct available ore, and active drones (ore/phase/cargo); GuiAsteroid/GuiDrone synced from the ECS each frame. CRITICAL: the read-only Explore agent that mapped the landscape gave a useful map BUT its CODE sketch had real bugs (passing &mut world into a method while a query_mut held it; despawn-during-iteration; world.get_mut vs this hecs's world.get::<&mut T>) — all avoided in the real impl (verified 1st-principles, not trusted). PROVEN by mining::drone_tests (commission iron drone -> 18 ticks -> >=8 iron_ore_0 delivered + drone despawned + the 8-ore asteroid deleted; + 'commission for an ore no asteroid has launches nothing'). VERIFIED: 368/368 native lib + relay cargo check clean + DroneSystem registered & not-deferred (lint passes by inspection; its binary LNK1318s locally, CI runs the real lint). #5b DEFERRED (tracked in gameplay-loops.md): SERVER-AUTHORITATIVE MMO asteroids (relay GameWorld; this loop is single-player native ECS) + the swarm + ABANDONED-deletion; 3D asteroid voxel visuals (terrain::asteroid octree exists but the loop is logical-only) + actual drone flight/position; nickel/platinum refine recipes so mined ore feeds the crafting tree.
recent_decisions.why: Build order #5 (operator-approved) — the most detailed mechanic in the operator's brain-dump (drones, finite asteroids, MMO swarm, delete-on-depleted/abandoned, iron/nickel/platinum, trip time, deliver-home-to-process). Scoped via a read-only Explore agent first (it is the biggest increment), then built from its MAP but VERIFIED its code rather than trusting it (it had borrow bugs). Kept the loop single-player + logical (an OreDeposit-style finite resource, no octree/flight) so it's testable now; the MMO + 3D layers are the logged #5b. Reused the SAME GUI->ECS bridge as #2/#3/#4 (the pattern is paying off). Used intent-collection for the cross-entity mutations because mining mutates the drone AND the asteroid AND (on deliver) the player inventory — three entities one query can't borrow at once.

recent_decisions.decision: GAMEPLAY-LOOP ARC #4 SHIPPED v0.331.0 — gardening loop (plant -> grow -> harvest -> food). FarmingSystem was ALREADY registered + growing CropInstance entities from game_time + water/health (the engine-wiring arc had wired plant_registry + game_time); the loop was OPEN at both ends — no way to START a crop (plant) or REAP one (harvest). #4 closed both, reusing the #2/#3 GUI->ECS bridge. FarmingSystem.tick now drains four channels before its growth pass: plant_request (seed item id) -> map seed_<id>_0 to a plant id, consume 1 seed from the player, spawn CropInstance{first_stage, planted_at=now, water 1.0, health 100}; water_request (entity bits) -> water_level=1.0 + a little health; dev_grow_crops (bool) -> instantly mature every living crop (a testing affordance like 'Dev: stock all materials', so the loop is verifiable in seconds instead of game-days); harvest_request (entity bits) -> if mature, yield a qty in [yield_min,yield_max] of the produce item into the player + despawn the crop. Added yield_min/yield_max to PlantDef/PlantRow (from plants.csv). seed->plant->produce mapping is by convention (seed_<id>_0 -> <id> -> vegetable_/fruit_/grain_<id>_0) VALIDATED against item_registry so a harvest only ever yields an item that exists; warns if a plant has no produce item. GUI: inventory detail panel gains a Plant button on seed items (subcategory 'seed' / seed_ prefix); a new Garden panel lists crops (name/stage + growth/water/health bars, Water + Harvest buttons) + a 'Dev: grow all' button; GuiCrop synced from the ECS each frame. PROVEN by farming::gardening_tests (plant seed_tomato_0 -> one 'tomato' crop, seed consumed; dev-grow -> stage 'ripe'; harvest -> >=2 vegetable_tomato_0 in inventory + crop despawned) + a seed/harvest id-mapping unit test. VERIFIED: 366/366 native lib + relay cargo check clean. NO engine_wiring_lint change (FarmingSystem was already registered; the 4 gardening channels are command channels, not tracked registries). Closes the chain garden -> harvest -> cook -> eat -> vitals/buffs end to end. #4b DEFERRED (tracked in gameplay-loops.md): a data-driven harvest_item column on plants.csv (124/129 plants have no produce item in items.csv, so only ~5 yield on harvest today); soil/water-source entities + tilling/irrigation; 3D crop placement + visuals (crops are logical ECS entities now, not yet placed/rendered in the world).
recent_decisions.why: Build order #4 (operator-approved 'develop the gameplay loops as if 100% unlocked'). FarmingSystem's growth sim already ran but had no plant/harvest, so the loop was open at both ends. Closed it with the SAME GUI->ECS bridge as #2/#3 (no new pattern — consistency). Added 'Dev: grow all' because real growth takes game-days; the dev posture needs the loop testable in seconds (mirrors 'Dev: stock all materials'). Kept seed/produce mapping as a validated convention rather than a 129-row plants.csv edit for #4 velocity; the harvest_item column is the logged clean #4b follow-up. The production+survival chain now spans garden -> harvest -> cook -> eat -> vitals/buffs end to end.

recent_decisions.decision: GAMEPLAY-LOOP ARC #3 SHIPPED v0.330.0 — cooking + nutrition (the eat->vitals->buffs loop). REGISTERED the previously-deferred FoodSystem (off the engine_wiring_lint deferred list; 8 systems now tick). New ECS components Vitals{satiation,hydration,*_max} + StatusEffects{active:Vec<ActiveEffect{id,remaining}>} on the player. New StatusEffectRegistry loads status_effects.csv into the DataStore via the existing load_csv_registry helper, keeping effect durations/modifiers in data. FoodSystem.tick now does three passes: (1) EAT — drains a consume_request channel (the new Eat button on food items writes GuiState.pending_consume_item -> main-loop bridge), removes 1 item, restores satiation (calories_per_100g*0.15) + hydration (produce gives more), rolls raw_consumption_risk -> food_poisoning, a full meal -> well_fed; (2) DECAY — satiation/hydration fall each tick, low -> hungry/thirsty conditions (refreshed while held, cleared when recovered), empty -> starvation/dehydration Health drain, timed effects expire; (3) the original spoilage. COOKING needs no new mechanic — it is the existing crafting cooking recipes (cook_soup/cook_bread/...) producing safer + more-satiating food, already craftable via #2. Typed the nutrition_profiles (were opaque ron::Value -> NutritionProfile{id,category,calories_per_100g,raw_consumption_risk}); item->profile via a prefix classifier mirroring the existing spoilage one. Inventory page shows satiation/hydration bars + active-effect chips. PROVEN by food::nutrition_tests (eat cooked canned_food -> satiation+hydration up, item consumed, no poison; satiation 10 -> hungry, then 0 -> starvation Health drain) + status_effects::tests (real-CSV parse, durations come from data, modifier parsing). VERIFIED: 364/364 native lib + relay cargo check clean + engine_wiring_lint assertions confirmed by inspection (register(FoodSystem) present, FoodSystem out of DEFERRED, status_effect_registry wired, no new impl System). The lint BINARY can't link locally — LNK1318 PDB 'LIMIT (12)' on the full HumanityOS.exe bin, a known Windows-MSVC-only issue that debug=0 does NOT fix; CI/Linux runs the real lint on push. #3b DEFERRED (tracked in gameplay-loops.md, not silent): status-effect MODIFIER CONSUMPTION (no system reads the stat:value:op mods yet, so buffs are visible but not mechanically applied) — wire PlayerControllerSystem(speed)+renderer(vision); night_vision-from-vitamins; a Drink action (+water items); spoilage->nutrition-loss on eat; data-driven item->profile link (items.csv column).
recent_decisions.why: Operator's vision: 'cook or eat raw... baseline satiated/hydrated + buffs (move/reload/night vision); possible harm.' The DATA was already deep but UNUSED: food_system.ron has 12 nutrition profiles WITH raw_consumption_risk (0.40 raw meat -> 0.0 cooked), and status_effects.csv has well_fed/hungry/thirsty/food_poisoning with stat:value:op modifiers — but FoodSystem was a spoilage-only scaffold that loaded nutrition as opaque ron::Value and never used it. So #3 = WIRE the existing data into a real loop (register FoodSystem + add Vitals + consume bridge), not write a new system — same posture as the whole engine-wiring/gameplay arc. Reused the #1+#2 GUI->ECS bridge verbatim. Held buff MODIFIER CONSUMPTION as #3b because it touches movement + the renderer (a separate, larger surface); the eat/decay/conditions/poison/well_fed loop + visible effects is complete + automated-test-proven now without it (no-bandaid: the gap is logged, not hidden).

recent_decisions.decision: GAMEPLAY-LOOP ARC opened (operator vision brain-dump + 'develop as if 100% unlocked'). Wrote docs/design/gameplay-loops.md = the holistic map (survival needs -> production chain -> connective systems -> threats -> progression-LAST), noting ~40 systems already exist in code (the engine_wiring_lint deferred list) so the work is WIRING loops + spawning content + glue, NOT writing systems. Operator's core mechanics captured: drone->asteroid mining (finite resources, MMO swarm, delete-on-depleted/abandoned), nutrition (satiation/hydration baseline + buffs from variety/surplus, cooked>raw, debuffs/harm from deficiency), concise complete plant set (proposed potato/soybean/leafy-green/tomato/sunflower/carrot). Build order: 1 full-unlock dev provisioning, 2 real crafting loop, 3 cooking+nutrition, 4 gardening, 5 drone/asteroid mining, 6 refining-chain depth, 7 survival systems online, 8 progression layer LAST. ── SHIPPED increment #1+#2 v0.329.0 (the GUI->ECS command bridge, shared by every loop): GuiState gains pending_craft_recipe + dev_stock_materials; the main loop (before system_runner.tick) bridges them into DataStore Mutex channels (craft_request, dev_stock_materials); CraftingSystem.tick drains them in ONE exclusive world.query_mut pass -> finds the Controllable+Inventory player -> dev-stock fills one full stack of EVERY distinct recipe INPUT (raws AND intermediates) + grows the bag via new Inventory::ensure_slots so all ~85 inputs fit a default 36-slot inventory; craft_request -> request_craft -> REAL consume/produce on the player's ECS Inventory (which already syncs to GuiState.inventory_items at lib.rs:1473, so results show live). KEY CORRECTION vs the first cut: stocking only RAWS (inputs never an output) left every recipe needing an INTERMEDIATE uncraftable -- e.g. smelt_iron needs coal_0, itself a recipe output (charcoal->coal), so raws-only could never craft it in one click. dev-stock now provisions ALL inputs = every recipe craftable immediately = the literal 'unlocked 100%' posture. (The raws-only first cut also surfaced a hecs gotcha: world.query(...).iter() + world.get::<&mut Inventory> -- the query's shared borrow of the Inventory column stayed live so the &mut get failed silently; query_mut's exclusive access fixes it cleanly.) Crafting page: Craft button sets pending_craft_recipe (real, not the cosmetic queue) + a 'Dev: stock all materials' button. PROVEN GREEN by crafting_bridge_tests::dev_stock_then_gui_craft_request_runs_the_full_loop (real data: dev-stock -> request smelt_iron -> consumes exactly 2 iron_ore_0 + 1 coal_0 + produces iron_ingot_0; coal_0's presence asserts all-inputs stocking, not just raws). VERIFIED: 361/361 native lib + relay cargo check clean + engine_wiring_lint 2/2.
recent_decisions.why: Operator steered to the gameplay loops with a clear 'fully unlocked for dev' posture. Built the GUI->ECS command bridge FIRST because every loop (craft/cook/plant/mine) needs the UI to drive real ECS actions; the dev provisioning makes the chain testable immediately. Kept crafting logic IN the CraftingSystem (it has world + registries); the main loop only bridges GuiState<->DataStore -- clean separation, reused by all future loops.

recent_decisions.decision: Crafting page UX iteration (operator tested the v0.326 layout fix -> works much better; gave 2 follow-ups). v0.327.0 = COMPACT ROWS: each recipe 2 tight lines (name+time on line 1, ingredients on line 2) vs the old 3, inner_margin 8->4, gap 2->1 -> ~2x density (~13 -> ~25 visible). v0.328.0 = NESTED CATEGORY TREE: data/crafting/categories.json is now a HIERARCHY (top-level groups -> leaf categories); new load_crafting_category_groups + CraftCategoryGroup struct; GuiState selection changed craft_category(usize) -> craft_selected_category(Option<String>, None=All); crafting.rs sidebar = 'All' + collapsible egui CollapsingHeader groups (Production/Equipment/Construction/Sustenance/Technology) each holding leaf categories; recipes filter by the selected category name (case-insensitive). Fully data-driven / infinite-of-X (edit the JSON to add/reorganize). Both exes built+archived. DEEPER NESTING (subdivide WITHIN a category for thousands): leaves map to recipe.category, so going below the category = split it into finer recipe.category values + regroup in JSON -- a TAXONOMY call routed to the operator's domain (pure data; the tree auto-picks-up new categories, no code). cargo check native clean for each.
recent_decisions.why: Operator's hands-on crafting test surfaced density + navigation needs for thousands of recipes. Compaction (quick, no design dependency) shipped first; the tree (data-driven so the operator shapes the taxonomy) second. Kept the tree leaf = recipe.category (no mass re-tagging) -- deeper subdivision is a data/taxonomy effort that belongs to the operator's game-design knowledge.

recent_decisions.decision: v0.326.0 Crafting page LAYOUT FIX (operator's first hands-on test of the v0.325.0 recipe browser). The browser WAS populating correctly (Smelt Iron etc. + all categories) -- Wiring-1b worked -- but the page crammed the recipe-list + detail into a tiny ~200px top-left box (most of the screen wasted) and rows were only partially clickable. ROOT CAUSE: the page sized its content from ui.available_height() inside a single page-dispatched CentralPanel, and available_height under-reports badly in that nested context -> clamped to the .max(200) floor; the detail ScrollArea also auto-shrank to its content. FIX: rebuilt to the panel layout the WORKING Inventory page uses -- SidePanel::left categories | CentralPanel recipe-list (ScrollArea auto_shrink[false,false] = fills) | SidePanel::right detail (resizable 480px, fills) | TopBottomPanel::bottom queue strip. Each panel has a real bounded height so the scroll areas fill instead of collapsing. Whole-row click = frame.show().response.interact(click) + ui.set_width(available_width) (was just the inner text bbox). cargo check native clean; v0.326.0 exe rebuilt + archived. LESSON (durable): do NOT size page content from ui.available_height() in a page-dispatched CentralPanel -- it under-reports; use SidePanel/TopBottomPanel/CentralPanel (real bounded heights) + ScrollArea::auto_shrink([false,false]) to fill. Operator re-testing. Offered single-list vs multi-column grid as a follow-up.
recent_decisions.why: Operator's first hands-on test surfaced that system-wired + UI-populated still needs the UI to RENDER usably (system-wired != playable, again, one layer up). Fixed by matching the proven Inventory layout rather than patching the unreliable available_height math.

recent_decisions.decision: ENGINE-WIRING Wiring-1b SHIPPED v0.325.0 -- Crafting PAGE recipe browser populated + STATUS.md accuracy. Found Wiring-1 made the crafting SYSTEM work (test-proven) but the Crafting PAGE was still empty (GuiState.craft_recipes Vec never populated -> always 'No recipes match'). Added gui::load_crafting_recipes(data_dir) (parses recipes.csv -> GuiRecipe via the shared loader + Recipe::parse_ingredients; native-gated) + wired gui_state.craft_recipes at startup (lib.rs, beside the categories load). The page now lists 300+ real recipes with ingredients/outputs/station/time = a working recipe BROWSER. Corrected the qa item (browser, not 'craft something') + a STATUS.md accuracy note (line 292 'scaffolds registered+ticking' was FALSE -> unregistered; blanket note that systems-table check-marks = implemented not running, pointing at engine_wiring_lint; row-by-row downgrade spawn-tasked). VERIFIED 360 native lib tests (incl. load_crafting_recipes_populates_from_real_data) + relay clean; rebuilt the v0.325.0 exe. ── SESSION ARC STATE: engine-wiring FOUNDATION done across v0.320-0.325 (TURN P2P-complete / containers / MMO persistence / registries->crafting-works / game_time+enforcement-lint / recipe-browser). The VERIFIABLE high-value wiring is shipped + enforced. NEXT BIG ARC = the PLAYABLE LAYER (real crafting loop + item sources + content entities + register the 32 deferred systems as their entities spawn + interaction effects + NPC/dialogue) -- needs the OPERATOR's game vision (what's the core loop) + LIVE TESTING (I can't verify gameplay headless). Flagged to the operator. Also unblocked + verifiable WITHOUT their vision: DATA EXPANSION (the operator's original ask; schema now proven by working crafting/inventory).
recent_decisions.why: Closing the system-wired-vs-UI-visible gap honestly: Wiring-1 was true at the system level but the UI page was empty, which reads as 'crafting broken' to the operator. The browser is a real, verifiable, self-contained win; the craft button + full loop are deferred honestly (no item source exists, so wiring the button now would operate on nothing). Surfaced the playable-layer-needs-operator-vision boundary rather than blindly building gameplay I cannot live-verify.

recent_decisions.decision: ENGINE-WIRING Wiring-2 + Wiring-3 (bundling as v0.324.0) + PDB build fix. Wiring-2 = game_time EXPORT: TimeSystem now writes its advanced GameTime into the DataStore each frame behind a Mutex (the interaction_prompt interior-mutability pattern, since System::tick only gets &DataStore); the 5 readers (farming/ecology/hydrology/weather + the lib.rs HUD) lock+read it; seeded at world init in lib.rs. Before this, TimeSystem never exported (despite its doc), so every time-dependent system saw Spring/elapsed=0 (the audit's crops-freeze + dead HUD clock). NATIVE only (wasm readers hit the absent slot + fall back exactly as before -> unchanged; full wasm wiring still tracked). Proven by game_time_export_tests (advances + exports; absent-slot is a safe no-op). Wiring-3 = THE ENFORCEMENT LINT (operator's 'do it right, like the theme system' core ask): tests/engine_wiring_lint.rs is the theme_token_lint analog for the engine -- every `impl System for X` under src/systems/ must be REGISTERED in src/lib.rs OR listed in DEFERRED_SYSTEMS with a written reason (build fails otherwise); + a core_runtime_data_stays_wired check that lib.rs keeps loading item/recipe/plant/container registry + game_time. Catalogs the REAL state honestly: 7 registered (Time/Player/Interaction/Farming/Inventory/Container/Crafting), 32 deferred each WITH a reason. The list shrinks as systems get genuinely wired -- same discipline as LEGACY_OFFENDERS. PDB NOTE (local-Windows-DEV only, blocks NOTHING that ships): the full native HumanityOS.exe DEV link hits LNK1318 'LIMIT (12)' (MSVC incremental PDB over limit) -- surfaced when the lint's cargo test built the bin. CI/Linux has no PDB + release/build-game (debug=false) is unaffected. Tried [profile.dev] debug=1, did NOT fix it -> REVERTED (don't ship a non-fix). Verified the lint anyway by running its test BINARY directly from target/debug/deps/ (both tests pass); local-dev workarounds = cargo test --lib + direct test-bin runs + cargo check. Deeper PDB fix (debug=0 / split-debuginfo) deferred -- low value since CI+release already work. Wiring-4 ANALYSIS (-> DEFERRED, cataloged not blindly wired): player MOVEMENT IS NOT BROKEN -- it's camera-driven (renderer CameraController), independent of the dead ECS PlayerControllerSystem (which needs BOTH physics_world in the DataStore AND a PhysicsBody on the player -- it has neither). InteractionSystem prompts work but the E-key handler only log::debug!s (no dispatch). So physics-based movement + real interaction effects are GAMEPLAY FEATURES (need content entities + handlers + live verification I can't do now), not quick wiring fixes -- deferred honestly. NET RESULT: the verifiable, high-value engine wiring is done (registries + game_time live, enforced by a lint); the rest is transparently cataloged. DATA EXPANSION is now UNBLOCKED -- crafting/inventory exercise the item/recipe schema with working code, so the operator's original 'fill the DB' is now meaningful (schema proven stable) whenever they want it.
recent_decisions.why: Operator: 'do it all, no bandaids, like the theme/widgets system (enforced, one source of truth).' The lint IS that enforcement -- it makes 'systems exist but never tick' impossible to reintroduce silently and documents every deferral with a reason instead of hiding the gap or blindly registering systems whose live behaviour I can't verify against the player/NPC entities. The PDB fix is a real build remedy (not a workaround) that also unblocks the operator's local dev bin builds. Wiring-4 deferred because movement already works (camera) and interaction effects need a design + content layer -- doing them blind would be the bandaid.

recent_decisions.decision: ENGINE-WIRING Wiring-1 SHIPPED v0.323.0 -- registries -> DataStore (the keystone unlock). Added ItemRegistry::from_csv / RecipeRegistry::from_csv / PlantRegistry::from_csv (each parses the real schema via the shared assets::loader::parse_csv, reusing the existing Recipe::parse_ingredients for pipe-separated item:qty; colon-separated growth_stages/seasons for plants). lib.rs world-load: replaced the load-then-discard (let _ = load_csv) -- the audit's central bug -- with a graceful load_csv_registry helper that fs::reads from the data dir (so modding works) -> from_csv -> data_store.insert, mirroring the container_registry pattern. PROVEN: a NEW end-to-end test crafting_end_to_end_tests::real_recipes_load_and_a_timed_craft_produces_output loads the REAL items.csv+recipes.csv (include_bytes), stocks iron_ore_0:2+coal_0:1, requests smelt_iron, ticks past the 10s timer, asserts iron_ingot_0 produced + inputs consumed; + 3 hermetic from_csv unit tests. NATIVE only; wasm can't fs::read -> wasm registries via embedded data is a tracked follow-up (wasm registers the systems but they no-op gracefully without data). NEXT: Wiring-2 = game_time export (Mutex<GameTime> in DataStore, TimeSystem writes it first each frame, ~4 reader systems lock+clone) so crops grow + seasons drive sims + register the dormant sim systems (weather/ecology/hydrology/atmosphere/disasters -- test_*_ticks_without_panic confirms they're safe). Then Wiring-3 = enforcement lint (orphaned-data + implemented-but-unregistered-system test, the theme_token_lint analog).

recent_decisions.decision: inc-3b TURN SHIPPED v0.320.0 -> native P2P NAT-traversal arc COMPLETE (inc-1 DataChannel / inc-2 group mesh / inc-3a STUN srflx / inc-3b TURN).
