recent_decisions.decision: GAME-CODE AUDIT (operator redirect: 'see what we have game code feature-wise before filling out massive amounts of data as schema may change'). 3 read-only Explore agents mapped the real runtime state. KEY FINDING (3 agents converged): the game is extensively WRITTEN but barely WIRED. The runtime DataStore is ~99pct empty -- only 5 keys inserted (input_state, camera_*, interaction_prompt). lib.rs:587-589 loads items/plants/recipes CSV via 'let _ = load_csv(...)' and DISCARDS the result; no ItemRegistry/RecipeRegistry/PlantRegistry is ever inserted into the DataStore -> every system that reads data.get(reg) gets None and silently no-ops. Only 6 systems registered+ticked (Time, PlayerController, Interaction, Farming, Inventory, Crafting); ~35 systems (weather, ecology, hydrology, atmosphere, disasters, quests, skills, AI, combat, vehicles, electrical, medical...) exist as real code (some 300-1200 LOC) but are NEVER registered = dead code. Of the 6 ticking: crafting can't craft (no recipe_registry), farming crops freeze (no plant_registry + TimeSystem never EXPORTS game_time to DataStore so all time-dependent systems fall back to Spring/t=0), player ECS-physics path dead (physics_world never in DataStore), interaction E-key only logs. The ONE genuinely-working gameplay system is Inventory (and it works by parsing items.csv DIRECTLY, bypassing the dead registry). STATUS.md/FEATURES.md OVERSTATE -- mark crafting/farming/weather/quests done when they're no-op/unregistered. CONCLUSION: operator's instinct right -- pouring data would be wasted (nothing consumes it; schemas shift as wiring forces real shape). REAL PRIORITY = WIRE THE ENGINE, holistically + enforced (theme-system standard): a consistent data-loading layer + a lint that fails the build on orphaned data / implemented-but-unregistered systems (the theme_token_lint analog), NOT a 3-registry bandaid. ARC ORDER: land TURN (done) -> merge container 6c8d8e5f (its container_registry IS correctly wired = the template) -> merge MMO de2fcf54 (reconcile Cargo.toml tokio signal vs sha1/md-5) -> engine-wiring arc (registries->DataStore + game_time export + register/feed systems + physics bridge + interaction dispatch + enforcement lint) -> reconcile docs to reality -> THEN data-fill (finally has consumers). Full per-domain audit tables in this session transcript.
recent_decisions.why: Operator explicitly redirected from data-breadth toward understanding code first; the audit proved data had already outrun working code (the container subagent's earlier item/recipe-registry-not-wired finding generalizes to the whole engine). Doing the wiring as an enforced architecture (not a patch) is the operator's 'no bandaids, like the theme/widgets system' bar -- a lint makes the fix non-regressable.

recent_decisions.decision: Concurrency arc continued (v0.307.0 + v0.308.0) + velocity directive persisted. Operator gave an emphatic working-norm directive: stop producing checkpoints/recaps/permission-asks, do EVERYTHING, move as fast as possible, no arbitrary delays except them being away from the keyboard, recaps come from logs not replies. Persisted to CLAUDE.md 'Working norm' + new memory feedback_velocity_no_checkpoints.md (correctness still required — 'fast' = no artificial stops, not skipping verification; cost is a flat Max sub with abundant headroom so lean on parallel agents). Then shipped, parallel (me on client + background agents on relay, split by feature-flag-disjoint trees so neither breaks the other's compile): v0.307.0 = PIN-unlock off-thread (mirrors v0.306 passphrase-unlock) + clipboard image upload off-thread (grab on main, upload POST on worker) + SQLite read-pool FOUNDATION (r2d2 8 read-only conns + opt-in with_read_conn; with_conn/with_conn_mut unchanged on the single writer; agent audited that with_conn is pervasively used for writes so kept them on the writer — the conservative correct call). v0.308.0 = migrated 57 provably-read-only hot paths to with_read_conn (DM list/load, members, signed-object reads, channel/message history, roles list, profiles, reactions, DID resolve). VERIFIED each agent's work before ship: reviewed diffs, ran the full suite (279/279), backstop-grepped for write-SQL inside any with_read_conn closure (none in production — only the deliberate rejection test). The read-only pool physically rejects mis-migrated writes (loud, not silent) — strong safety net. Correctly LEFT on the writer: all read-then-write closures + role_def (default-deny on pool-exhaustion would silently demote a capability check). EXE: build-game batched after the relay run (server-side changes redeploy via CI; one exe sync at the end of the concurrency batch).
recent_decisions.why: Operator wants maximum forward velocity with no artificial stops, but still requires double-checking subagent work. Resolved by parallelizing across feature-flag-disjoint trees (gui builds exclude relay and vice versa, so a background relay agent and my client edits can't break each other's compile), and by leaning on the read-only-pool's physical write-rejection + the 279-test gate as the verification backbone for the DB-layer changes (the highest-risk surface). Batched the exe build because relay-only changes have no native-visible effect (desktop uses the VPS relay) — building an exe per relay release would be an arbitrary delay with no operator-testable payoff; one build-game after the relay batch keeps the local exe in sync per SOP.

recent_decisions.decision: Parallel concurrency batch (v0.306.0): vault-unlock off-thread (me) + relay verify-offload (background agent), both verified before merge. Operator: 'work on what you can next in parallel, then tell me exactly what to test.' Split the work so the two fronts CANNOT collide: I did C1 vault-unlock in src/gui/ while a background agent did R1+R2 relay verify-offload in src/relay/ — and crucially the agent builds `--features relay` which EXCLUDES the gui, so neither side could break the other's compile. C1 (src/gui/widgets/passphrase_modal.rs + mod.rs): the 600k-iter PBKDF2 decrypt (+ the legacy 100k→600k re-encrypt, a 2nd heavy PBKDF2) now run on a worker thread with an 'Unlocking…' spinner; cheap post-steps (keychain stash, apply_pq_identity, save) stay on main; new apply_unlock_outcome() holds the post-decrypt logic verbatim; new GuiState passphrase_unlocking + passphrase_unlock_rx (native-gated, mirrors p2p_group_loader). Covers the main unlock path; draw_change/draw_pin_unlock/draw_pin_change still inline (tracked follow-up). R1+R2 (agent, src/relay/api_v2_objects.rs + relay.rs): object-ingest + chat-message ML-DSA verify moved to spawn_blocking; the DUPLICATE ingest verify removed (post_object verified then put_signed_object verified again). I VERIFIED the agent's work before merge (not on trust): reviewed both diffs; confirmed put_signed_object verifies FIRST and returns Err before any INSERT (signed_objects.rs:72) so bad-sig is never stored; confirmed the IngestError status-code classification matches the real error-message markers ('signature verification failed' → 401, 'author_pubkey must be' → 400); confirmed chat verify is .await.unwrap_or(false) = FAIL CLOSED. Integrated tree: native compiles, 277/277 relay tests pass (incl. put_rejects_tampered_object + identify_challenge + federation forged/tampered rejection), 6/6 pbkdf2 round-trip tests pass, theme/glyph lints green. Shipped v0.306.0; local exe v0.306.1; relay redeploys via CI. concurrency-roadmap.md updated (C1, R1, R2 → done; tails tracked).
recent_decisions.why: The operator asked for parallel progress + a test list. Splitting by feature-flag-disjoint trees (gui vs relay) let me genuinely run a background agent while I worked, with zero compile-collision risk (relay build excludes gui). Took the security-critical vault-unlock myself; let the agent do the (also careful but more mechanical) relay async-offload, then verified it the way the operator requires — which confirmed the agent preserved every security gate (bad-sig rejection, fail-closed chat verify, status codes). Both are 'move existing-correct logic to a different execution context' = high-value, low-logic-risk, strongly test-covered. Vault-unlock PIN/change flows + the lower-volume relay verify sites are deferred tails (tracked in concurrency-roadmap.md), not rushed.

recent_decisions.decision: BUDGET MODEL CORRECTED + first verified parallel-implementation batch (v0.305.0). Operator corrected a stale assumption I'd cited TWICE as a reason to scope down: they're on Claude Max FLAT $100/mo, had used only ~6% of the weekly allotment (resetting within the hour) — abundant headroom; usage is a subscription rate-limit, NOT per-token dollars. Updated memory project_financial_context.md: stop citing 'token/agent cost / your budget' as a scoping reason; the real gates on 'do everything' are (a) correctness/verifiability (I ship only what I can double-check; visual/UI still needs operator eyes) and (b) file-conflict discipline (parallel agents need disjoint scope / worktrees). Kept the separate long-term 'architect for Claude's eventual absence' principle (different timescale). THEN acted on the standing 'do everything incl backlog' with cost cleared: launched 2 concurrent scoped subagents on DISJOINT files — (1) docs-only: refreshed the 200-versions-stale STATUS.md (v0.132→v0.304, reframed as inventory deferring to PRIORITIES, added a crypto reality-check matching CLAUDE.md) + swept dead pre-v0.90 path refs across 13 docs; (2) tests-only: 11 relay storage integration tests (DM envelope/roundtrip, mutual-follow DM gate, channel persistence). VERIFIED everything before shipping (operator's rule): confirmed only-expected-files changed (no scope violations), 277/277 relay tests pass, native compiles, STATUS crypto claims match canonical. The tests agent surfaced a REAL user-facing bug — get_dm_conversations counted MY OUTBOUND DMs as unread instead of the partner's inbound (placeholder ?1..?M=my_ph/to_key, ?M+1..=p_ph/from_key, but bound partner-keys-first → inverted from/to). I confirmed it by walking the numbered-placeholder binding, FIXED it (bind my-keys-first, matching the last_msg query above), and tightened the test to assert unread=2 (regression lock). Also fixed FEATURES.md DM crypto drift (ECDH→Kyber768). Shipped v0.305.0; local exe v0.305.1. The concurrent agents shared the working tree (no worktree isolation) but disjoint scopes held — Agent1 even observed Agent2's in-progress .rs writes and correctly left them alone.
recent_decisions.why: The budget correction is a durable operator correction → persisted immediately to memory (the cross-session rule). With cost off the table, the right move is MORE parallelism + MORE verification, not less — but bounded to autonomously-verifiable work (docs I cross-check, tests I run); visual UI (bloom, glossary widget) still deferred for operator eyes. Verifying the subagent output rather than trusting it caught the agents' good work AND let me upgrade a 'documented bug' into a shipped fix + regression test — exactly the double-check the operator asked for, and the higher-value outcome (a real DM-unread-badge bug fixed) than just adding tests. Deferred (own careful passes, tracked in docs/design/concurrency-roadmap.md): vault-unlock PBKDF2 off-thread (C1, worst freeze), relay verify→spawn_blocking + SQLite read-pool (R1-R4, live concurrency surface).

recent_decisions.decision: Multithreading audit (4 parallel read-only agents) + first client wins (v0.304.0). Operator asked 'what else can we multithread' then 'do everything incl. backlog, spin up subagents, double-check before upload.' Pushed back (candor) on literal 'everything': blind whole-backlog fan-out can't be verified to the no-bandaids bar, parallel agents editing shared files (GuiState/mod.rs) cause the context-rot corruption CLAUDE.md warns about, and a speculative fan-out is real $ on a tight budget. Instead ran a SAFE parallel READ-ONLY audit: 4 general-purpose agents mapped (1) UI-thread blockers, (2) engine parallelism, (3) relay concurrency, (4) backlog triage — findings captured in NEW docs/design/concurrency-roadmap.md (file:line grounded, so the audit never re-runs). Then shipped the client-only low-risk subset v0.304.0: group message SEND now backgrounds the POST (was blocking every send) + optimistic echo; killed a per-poll Dilithium+Kyber keygen in replace_p2p_messages (profile_public_key already IS the dilithium hex → cheap hex+blake3); removed a dead synchronous first-render refresh_p2p_groups (the v0.303.0 background list refresh preempts it). DELIBERATELY DEFERRED with reasons: vault-unlock PBKDF2 off-thread (C1 — worst freeze ~200ms-1s but security-critical login path → own tested pass); relay verify→spawn_blocking + duplicate-verify removal + SQLite read-pool (R1-R4 — live security/concurrency surface → own tested passes, NOT rushed into a mixed turn); engine/terrain rayon parallelism (Tier-2 — audit proved it's LATENT: heavy systems aren't wired into the live runner + terrain isn't sampled per-vertex live, so it'd optimize code that doesn't run = premature). cargo native+relay green, lints green, group_e2ee 2/2, KAT pass. Backlog triage: 10 safe-autonomous / 7 need-operator-decision / 15 multi-week / 6 blocked (see roadmap doc + the audit).
recent_decisions.why: The operator's 'double-check before uploading' is the constraint that makes 'everything at once' self-limiting — I can only ship what I can verify to this project's bar. So: parallelize the SAFE part (read-only audit, no file conflicts, far cheaper than implementation fan-out), bank the unambiguous client wins, and route the security/concurrency-sensitive + live-server items to their own careful passes rather than a risky mega-commit. Tier-2 engine parallelism is correctly deferred because parallelizing not-yet-live code is premature optimization (measure first). This protects the operator's budget AND the mission's correctness bar while still moving fast on what's verifiable.

recent_decisions.decision: Native P2P groups: async + cog menu + auto-switch on create (v0.303.0). Four operator issues from testing v0.302.x. (1) 'Can't leave/disband in app' — ROOT CAUSE was UI overflow, NOT a crypto/auth bug (verified subject==author for self-leave: derive_pq_identity and submit_signed_object use the identical dilithium derivation, so the relay authorizes it). The Leave/Disband ghost buttons were crammed into the group header row (Back + name + 'end-to-end encrypted' + Copy invite + Leave + Disband) and clipped off the right edge on a narrow center panel. FIX: moved Copy invite / Leave / Disband into a COG + right-click popup on the left-rail P2P row (egui popup_below_widget, deferred-action vars applied after the loop) — mirrors the web right-click menu AND the legacy native group cog AND the operator's original 'cog→settings popup' mental model. Header trimmed to Back + name + encrypted + Copy invite. (2) 'App didn't update when I disbanded/left via web (still 2 users, chat persisted)' — native never refreshed the list/roster after initial load. FIX: background list refresh every ~6s (spawn_groups_list_refresh) keeps the rail + counts fresh; if the open group vanished (disbanded/left elsewhere) it exits to #general + drops the decrypted history. (3) 'Switching groups hangs ~1s, web instant' — the load did 4-6 blocking ureq calls on the click frame. FIX: ALL group net/crypto now on a BACKGROUND THREAD — api_v2::load_group_blocking returns a GroupLoad struct over an mpsc channel; drain_p2p_loaders applies it on the main thread. Click switches the view INSTANTLY (header + 'Loading…'); messages fill in when the worker returns; the ~4s periodic reload no longer freezes. Replaced synchronous enter_p2p_group/poll_p2p_group with spawn_group_load/apply_group_load/spawn_groups_list_refresh/drain_p2p_loaders. New native-gated GuiState: p2p_group_loader (String,Receiver<GroupLoad>), p2p_groups_list_loader (Receiver<Vec<P2pGroupInfo>>), p2p_group_loading bool. (4) 'Auto-switch to group on create to force keygen' — both clients now switch into the new group after create (native create modal + web createP2pGroup→openP2pGroup). cargo check native+relay green; theme+glyph lints green; web syntax OK; NO relay/schema change. Shipped v0.303.0 (CI auto-deploys web on push); local exe v0.303.1.
recent_decisions.why: Operator hit the hang + couldn't reach leave/disband + saw stale state after cross-client changes. The hang and the staleness share one root cause — synchronous ureq on the UI thread — so the correct fix is to move group I/O off-thread (mpsc + try_recv, the standard egui async pattern), which fixes BOTH and brings native to web parity (instant switch). Diagnosed the leave/disband 'bug' as header overflow, NOT broken crypto (ruled out by confirming subject==author) — so the fix is UI placement (a cog/right-click popup), which also matches how the operator described it originally and how web + legacy native already work. Chose threading over the cheaper 'defer enter to next frame' because the operator explicitly wants web-parity instant + the periodic refresh must not freeze either — do-it-right over a patch.

recent_decisions.decision: Crown icon on owned groups (v0.302.0). Operator: a tiny crown left of the group name marks groups I created vs joined. is_creator was already on every P2P group (GET /api/v2/groups, v0.301.0) so this was pure render. Web: new `crown` SVG in icons.js (band + 3 spikes, stroked like the rest of the line-art set); chat-social.js renders it for is_creator rows tinted with the --warning CSS var (gold). Native: new paint_crown in widgets/icons.rs (filled band rect + 3 convex triangle spikes — a concave crown polygon won't tessellate; takes a color param) painted at the left of creator rows in theme.warning() (the gold token — keeps theme_token_lint green, no hardcoded literal). Verified LIVE via curl on the deployed icons.js + chat-social.js?v=7. Both lints green. Also: confirmed v0.301.0 (leave/disband relay + disbanded migration) deployed LIVE via the CI pipeline (deploy.yml rsyncs web + rebuilds/restarts relay on every push; the v0.301.0 run succeeded 3m40s, so the migration ran on relay boot). My manual `just sync` was redundant with CI. Local exe v0.302.1.
recent_decisions.why: Small directed UX ask, done cleanly: reused the existing is_creator signal (no new endpoint), and crucially used the theme's gold token (theme.warning / --warning) rather than a hardcoded crown color, so it stays themeable and doesn't grow the theme_token_lint debt — the disciplined choice the lint exists to enforce. Verified live by fetching the public assets (read-only), NOT by querying prod (an ad-hoc prod SSH read was correctly blocked by the auto-mode classifier as out-of-scope; the project's own `just status` + a public-asset curl gave the same confirmation safely).

recent_decisions.decision: Delete-groups shipped both clients (v0.301.0): Leave (anyone) + Disband (creator). Operator confirmed P2P groups work end-to-end (created on app+web, cross-invited, chatted both ways) then asked for a way to delete groups (list only grew). Built it sovereign-signed-object style, NOT a relay RPC: (1) LEAVE — relaxed index_group_member authorization in groups_p2p.rs so a member may action='remove' THEMSELVES (subject == author) even if not the creator; reuses the existing group_member_v1 type (no new wire format). Web buildGroupMemberV1 already existed → leaveP2pGroup; native submit_group_leave. (2) DISBAND — new group_disband_v1 signed object; index_group_disband verifies author == creator and sets a new p2p_groups.disbanded flag; p2p_groups_for_member filters disbanded=0 so it drops off EVERY member's list. The signed object is the durable P2P-replicable tombstone; index_group uses INSERT OR IGNORE so re-indexing group_v1 never un-disbands. Schema: disbanded added to CREATE (fresh) + guarded idempotent ALTER (live DB already had p2p_groups; DEFAULT 0 keeps existing groups live — followed the CLAUDE.md migration-ordering lesson). (3) CREATOR-AWARENESS — GET /api/v2/groups now returns is_creator per group (server-computed via new p2p_group_creator_fp helper + author_fingerprint of the caller) so Disband shows ONLY to the creator (never a silent no-op); native P2pGroupInfo gained is_creator. UI: web right-click menu (Leave / Disband-for-everyone, both confirm); native header Leave + Disband buttons next to Copy invite; both return to #general + refresh the list on action. Wired index_group_disband into put_signed_object. 4 new relay tests (self-leave drops member + group still exists for creator; non-creator can't evict someone else; creator disband hides for all; non-creator disband ignored) → 13/13 groups_p2p. group_e2ee 2/2, KAT PASS, native+relay compile. Relay change → minor bump v0.301.0; `just sync` rebuilt the VPS; local exe v0.301.1.
recent_decisions.why: Operator's explicit ask ('no way to delete groups, list getting bigger'). Did it RIGHT per the mission norm: a group is a set of signed objects, so leave/disband must also be signed objects that replicate P2P — not a relay-only delete that would vanish when the relay is bypassed (Phase 3+). Leave reuses group_member_v1 (minimal surface); disband is a new tombstone type. Self-leave authorization (subject==author) is the clean rule: you can always exit a group you're in, no creator needed. is_creator computed server-side avoids an extra per-group fetch on the client AND prevents the bad UX of showing Disband to someone whose disband the relay would silently ignore. Deferred the epoch-key-bootstrap polish (creator auto-rekey from the group LIST so a fresh joiner doesn't see 'No epoch key yet' until the creator opens the group) — it self-heals today and is a smoothness nicety, not a correctness bug; noted for later.

recent_decisions.decision: P2P group conversation is now a CHANNEL, not a modal — both clients (web v0.299.2, native v0.300.0). Operator tested v0.299.x and reported: (web) clicking a group hangs ~2s then opens a modal that lands behind its own darkening backdrop (unclickable), and 'why are we popping up an entirely new chat system? switching from #general to a group should be like changing #general → #announcements'; ALSO (web) groups don't appear on first load, only after interacting; (native) same modal problem + it's a separate chat UI. FIXES: (1) WEB — killed openP2pGroupDialog modal; new openP2pGroup(id,name) switches the main chat center panel to the group like switchChannel does (window.activeP2pGroup; messages render via the standard addChatMessage; composer monkey-patch routes sends to the group; switchChannel/openDmConversation/openGroup patched to closeP2pGroup; 4s poll). Invite via header link OR right-click context menu (small dismiss-on-outside popover, no full-bleed backdrop). View switch is instant (network load runs in background after the switch). (2) WEB first-load bug — loadP2pGroups set _p2pGroupsFetched=true BEFORE the myKey guard, so if it ran pre-identity the flag stuck true and never retried until create/join. Fixed: flag set inside loadP2pGroups only on a real attempt; renderGroupList gates the lazy trigger on myKey; connect() proactively loads groups after identity ready. (3) NATIVE — removed draw_p2p_group_modal entirely; P2P group = new chat_active_channel prefix 'p2pgroup:<id>' joining the existing dm:/group:/scratchpad dispatch. enter_p2p_group (full sync on click: rekey-if-creator + epoch fetch + roster fp→name/key map + decrypt) and poll_p2p_group (light 4s re-decrypt) project GroupMessages into chat_messages tagged with the channel so the standard renderer handles them (identicons/grouping/theme reused). Header: Back + green name + 'end-to-end encrypted' label + 'Copy invite' button (mints 7-day ticket to clipboard — no modal). Composer routes p2pgroup sends over HTTP (group_msg_v1) gated OUT of the WS block, with optimistic echo. Left-rail rows highlight active; empty state shows no-key/no-msg hint not the raw channel id. Dropped 🔒 (U+1F512 tofus in egui font) → added to icon_glyph_lint BROKEN_GLYPHS; E2EE signalled in words. State churn (mod.rs): removed show_p2p_group_modal/p2p_group_open_id/p2p_group_modal_ticket/p2p_group_chat_messages/p2p_group_chat_compose; added p2p_group_active_id/last_fetch/fp_to_key/fp_to_name/invite_status; kept epoch+epoch_key. cargo check native+relay green; group_e2ee 2/2; group-object KAT PASS; icon_glyph + theme lints green. Shipped v0.300.0; local exe v0.300.1.
recent_decisions.why: Operator was explicit twice: the group chat must be the SAME interface as server channels (switch like #general → #announcements), not a bolt-on modal with a parallel chat system — and the modal kept hitting an egui-style z-order bug where the backdrop covered it. Removing every full-bleed darkening overlay (web AND native) eliminates the bug CLASS rather than patching it. The native fix mirrors the web one structurally (a group is just another active-channel prefix), so the two clients now share one mental model. The first-load fix was a real correctness bug (flag burned before identity). Deferred: native enter does redundant blocking HTTP (brief hitch) — left as a noted follow-up rather than threading it now, because correctness + the inline UX were the operator's asks; threading is an optimization to do only if the hitch annoys in testing.

recent_decisions.decision: Phase 2 cross-client COMPLETE (v0.299.0) — both clients can now interop end-to-end on the same group object. Two pieces landed in one ship: (1) web rekey-on-join: chat-groups-p2p.js rekeyIfCreatorNeeds — when the creator opens a group dialog and the roster has new members not covered by the current epoch_key recipients, auto-mint a fresh epoch sealed to the full roster (forward secrecy on rotation). Wired into refresh() before the epoch-key fetch. parseGroupEpochKeyPayload exposed in pq-object.js to read existing recipients. (2) native chat-in-groups: new src/net/group_e2ee.rs (epoch-key sealing via ML-KEM-768 -> BLAKE3-KDF -> AES-256-GCM, AES-GCM per message — byte-identical to web's pq-object.js helpers; 2 round-trip tests pass: group_e2ee_roundtrip + wrong_recipient_cannot_open_epoch_key). src/net/api_v2.rs gains submit_group_epoch_key_v1 + submit_group_msg_v1 + GroupMessage struct + fetch_group_epoch_payload + fetch_and_decrypt_group_messages + fetch_group_members + fetch_signed_object + rekey_if_creator_needs + fetch_my_epoch_key + create_group_and_first_invite now issues the initial epoch key sealed to creator. chat.rs draw_p2p_group_modal: full chat view (ScrollArea message list with You/short-hex labels + UTC time, TextEdit compose + Send button + Enter-to-send + refresh button); dialog open triggers a sync rekey + key fetch + decrypt. GuiState fields: p2p_group_chat_epoch, p2p_group_chat_epoch_key, p2p_group_chat_messages, p2p_group_chat_compose. KAT still locked (group-object-kat: PASS). cargo check --features native + --features relay both green. Shipped: v0.299.0 (Rust changes minor bump). Local exe: v0.299.1 (just build-game). Phase 2 is done; Phases 3-5 are the resilience tracks (P2P transport, relay-independence, mDNS/DHT) and don't block testability.
recent_decisions.why: Operator: finish implementing the features that have been discussed before I can properly test groups. The MUST items were (1) cross-identity test path (rekey-on-join, or two web identities can't read each other) and (2) native chat-in-groups (so the operator can actually test on his native build, not just web). Both shipped together because the new Rust crypto module group_e2ee.rs deserves to land with the UI it serves, and rekey + initial-epoch + send/receive are all the same loop. recent_decisions.decision: ARCHITECTURE FINDING (reported to operator, NOT yet acted on): Groups are RELAY-MEDIATED, not P2P, despite a native comment claiming P2P-under-the-hood. handle_group_create/join/msg (src/relay/handlers/msg_handlers.rs ~1757-1926) all hit the relay SQLite (state.db.join_group_by_invite etc.) and route group_msg through the relay. So if united-humanity.us is down, group create/join/messaging ALL break — the operators stated P2P intent is unrealized. Also the invite URL is doubly broken: native (chat.rs:948) generates https://united-humanity.us/chat/group/{group.id} but (a) joining needs the SEPARATE invite_code (group.id != invite_code; handle_group_create returns both) and (b) the web has NO /chat/group route -> 404. NOT fixing the URL yet because the correct fix depends on the P2P decision (true P2P => the invite becomes a connection ticket, no relay URL). Presented as a direction fork for the operator.
recent_decisions.why: Operator major concern: groups are supposed to be P2P; if the website goes down can people still join via a shared URL? Investigation shows no — current groups fully depend on the relay. This is a real design fork (build true P2P groups = major track) that gates the group-invite-URL fix.

recent_decisions.decision: Operator chose TRUE P2P GROUPS (via AskUserQuestion) over relay-mediated-fix or federated-fallback. Wrote docs/design/p2p-groups.md: a group becomes a signed object + append-only signed membership/message logs replicated peer-to-peer over the EXISTING WebRTC DataChannels (web/chat/chat-p2p.js); relays demoted to optional accelerators (signaling rendezvous / opportunistic cache / presence). Invite = signed connection TICKET (not a relay URL). E2EE via per-epoch group key (generalize the Kyber768 dual-seal in src/net/dm_pq.rs), re-keyed on membership change for forward secrecy. Grounded via a research agent: reuse chat-p2p DataChannels, voice-room N-mesh signaling template, signed contact card (relay-free bootstrap), signed_objects+gossip+latest-wins, dual-seal, applySyncBundle merge, signed_moderation_logs governance shape. THE core gap = relay-independent signaling (today webrtc_signal rides ONE relay, relay.rs ~987/5507) -> multi-relay failover + peer-assisted signaling + TURN/peer-relay for NAT. Phases P1 sovereign-data+working-ticket-invite (fixes the 404) -> P2 signed+E2EE msgs -> P3 P2P transport -> P4 relay-independence (the payoff) -> P5 mDNS/DHT. Added as TIER 2 #8. NOT started code — design awaits operator review of 3 open decisions (TURN, epoch-key vs per-msg KEM, relay-as-accelerator).
recent_decisions.why: Operator major concern: if united-humanity.us is down, can people still join groups via a shared URL? Investigation: no — groups fully relay-dependent today. Operator picked the resilience north star (true P2P). Per do-it-right/no-bandaids + the design-before-code rule, the correct first increment is a grounded design doc that BUILDS ON existing primitives, not immediate code.

recent_decisions.decision: P2P groups: locked the 3 open decisions (operator approved my recs) — TURN = operator-run + peer-as-TURN fallback; encryption = per-epoch group key (re-key on membership change, forward secrecy); relay = optional accelerator. Marked DECIDED in docs/design/p2p-groups.md. Then shipped Phase 1 slice (a) v0.292.0: the relay-side SOVEREIGN DATA MODEL. New module src/relay/storage/groups_p2p.rs defines the object-format spec (module doc-comment, the cross-language contract) for group_v1 (group_id = its own object_id, self-certifying) / group_member_v1 (append-only admit/remove, references group_id, Phase-1 creator-only authority) / group_msg_v1 (Phase 2, encrypted). index_group + index_group_member project into new p2p_groups + p2p_group_roster tables (the membership-log fold), wired into put_signed_object. Additive — the existing relay-mediated group path (social.rs/handlers) is untouched, so nothing breaks during migration. 3 tests pass incl. the security test that an admit signed by a NON-creator is rejected (valid signature, failed authorization). Built on the existing signed_objects substrate (canonical CBOR + BLAKE3 object_id + Dilithium3 via core::object::ObjectBuilder) — ZERO new crypto.
recent_decisions.why: Operator said move forward with the plan + greenlit starting Phase 1. Per do-it-right + the codebase contract-first convention (object_format.md), the first slice is the data model with the spec embedded as code doc + tests, additive so it cannot break the working path. Reused the signed-object framework rather than inventing — the design is implementable with no new crypto, just new object types + projections.

recent_decisions.decision: P2P groups Phase 1 slice (b) shipped v0.292.1: cross-language signed-object construction for the web. Found the real gap — the web had Dilithium signing + BLAKE3 but NO canonical-CBOR encoder, so it literally could not build a signed_object (blocked web votes/vouches/recovery too, all marked follow-up). Built web/shared/canonical-cbor.js (byte-exact match to src/relay/core/encoding.rs: RFC7049 length-first key sort, shortest-int headers, definite-length, no floats/tags) + web/shared/pq-object.js (buildSignedObject / buildGroupV1 / buildGroupMemberV1 producing the POST /api/v2/objects submission with zero-sig-placeholder signable bytes matching Rust signable_bytes). KAT-LOCKED: scripts/group-object-kat.mjs (just group-kat) vs groups_p2p.rs::group_v1_canonical_kat assert identical payload hex (a1646e616d65696b61742d67726f7570) + object_id (c909a8df...70f7db9b) for a fixed group_v1 input. JS matched Rust byte-for-byte on the first try. End-to-end chain now ready: web builds group_v1 -> POST /api/v2/objects (route exists, api_v2_objects::post_object) -> put_signed_object verifies + index_group projects to roster. Patch bump — the Rust change is #[cfg(test)] only, binary unchanged.
recent_decisions.why: Operator: get this done completely and properly. Per the codebase contract-first + KAT convention (pq-kat.mjs), the foundation for any web-built signed object is a canonical-CBOR encoder proven byte-identical to Rust — without it a web group object is unverifiable by the relay. Built generic (not group-specific) so it unblocks all web signed objects.

recent_decisions.decision: P2P groups Phase 1 slice (c) — invite + admission model (v0.293.0). Design call (dictated by the resilience goal, not asked): invite = creator-signed CAPABILITY with a secret commitment, so members join even when the creator is OFFLINE. group_invite_v1 (creator-signed, references group, payload {expires_at, secret_hash=BLAKE3(secret)}); the out-of-band ticket carries the secret; group_join_v1 (joiner-signed, references [group_id, invite_id], payload {secret}); the roster fold admits the join AUTHOR iff BLAKE3(secret)==invite.secret_hash AND now<expires_at AND the invite was creator-signed. Randos cannot join (no secret); expired invites rejected; invites from non-creators are not projected so joins against them fail. Relay: index_group_invite/index_group_join + p2p_group_invites table in groups_p2p.rs, wired into put_signed_object; 7 tests pass. Web: buildGroupInviteV1/buildGroupJoinV1 + encodeInviteTicket/decodeInviteTicket + randomInviteSecret in pq-object.js (uses the KAT-locked canonical encoder). max_uses deferred (needs coordination; expiry is coordination-free). Additive/dormant until the UI (d) creates these objects.
recent_decisions.why: Operator: do it completely + properly. Offline-join is required by the mission (relay/creator down should not block joining); a capability+secret-commitment invite is the standard correct pattern and avoids needing the admin online to admit. Expiry chosen over use-count for Phase 1 because expiry is deterministic/coordination-free across peers.

recent_decisions.decision: P2P groups Phase 1 slice (d) v0.294.0 — chat UI create+join flow; THE 404 IS FIXED. Relay: GET /api/v2/groups?pubkey=<hex> (api_v2_objects::my_p2p_groups) reads p2p_groups+roster projection (p2p_groups_for_member computes the fp from pubkey). Web: new chat-groups-p2p.js (IIFE, lazy dynamic-imports /shared/pq-object.js + the vendored noble bundle for blake3, signs with the chat identity via window.pqSignMessage(myDilithiumSecret,...)): createP2pGroup -> buildGroupV1 -> POST /api/v2/objects; createP2pInvite -> buildGroupInviteV1(7-day) -> ticket via encodeInviteTicket (shown in a copy dialog); joinP2pGroupByTicket -> decodeInviteTicket -> buildGroupJoinV1; loadP2pGroups -> GET. chat-social.js renderGroupList now renders P2P groups (click -> openP2pGroupDialog roster+invite) above legacy relay-mediated ones; promptCreateGroup/promptJoinGroup repointed to the P2P flow (old group_create/group_join WS path retired in the UI; legacy groups still render until migrated). index.html loads chat-groups-p2p.js after chat-social.js. NOT browser-tested (preview unavailable) but: relay compiles, KATs+tests pass, the submission shape matches Rust SignedObjectSubmission field-for-field, signable bytes use the same zero-sig placeholder + KAT-locked encoder, and signing reuses the proven identify Dilithium path — so create/POST/verify/project is sound by construction. Minor bump (relay binary changed: new route + read query).

recent_decisions.decision: Operator deleted the live legacy test group + left for a few hours: instructed work on what you can. So (e) migration is MOOT (no live group to migrate); retiring the legacy group code path waits until the operator verifies the new (d) UI (keep it as fallback). Proceeding AUTONOMOUSLY on PHASE 2 (E2EE group messages) — the agreed next track, encryption scheme already decided (per-epoch group key). Safe to build unsupervised because the relay side is store+serve OPAQUE ciphertext + membership-gate (relay never decrypts), additive/dormant (no client uses the new message path until the chat UI is wired, which is deferred until the operator can browser-verify). Phase 2 needs no new crypto: reuse dm_pq.rs (ML-KEM-768 -> BLAKE3-KDF -> AES-256-GCM) to seal one epoch key to each member, AES-GCM messages under it. Member Kyber pubs come from registered_names.kyber_public via get_kyber_public(dilithium_hex) (already exists) — enrich GET /api/v2/groups roster with kyber_pub so the web sender can seal.

The UI wiring (which needs browser verification) is deferred to when the operator is back.

recent_decisions.decision: PHASE 2 slice 1 (autonomous) — relay E2EE-group-message backend. New object types projected (groups_p2p.rs, wired into put_signed_object): group_epoch_key_v1 (creator-authorized; p2p_group_epochs table records which object holds epoch N) + group_msg_v1 (membership-gated via p2p_group_has_member; p2p_group_messages log). The relay stores/serves OPAQUE ciphertext only — it never decrypts (each member key copy is ML-KEM-sealed in the payload; messages AES-GCM under the epoch key). Read endpoints (api_v2_objects.rs): GET /api/v2/groups/{id}/members (roster + each member kyber_public from registered_names, for the sender to seal to), /messages (encrypted history, capped 200), /epoch (latest group_epoch_key_v1). 2 new tests (epoch_key_indexed_only_from_creator, only_member_messages_are_logged) — 9 groups_p2p tests pass. Additive/dormant — no client posts these yet; safe to ship unsupervised.
recent_decisions.why: Operator away + work-on-what-you-can. Relay-side E2EE message plumbing is the safe high-value autonomous slice: relay never decrypts (E2EE), membership-gated, additive, tested. recent_decisions.decision: Phase 2 web — E2EE group messages (v0.297.1). New: canonical-cbor.js decodeCanonicalCbor (parse the relay payloads back); pq-object.js randomEpochKey, buildGroupEpochKeyV1 (seals one 32-byte key to each members Kyber pub via the proven dm_pq scheme, payload = {epoch, recipients:[{fp,ek_ct,nonce,ct}]}), openGroupEpochKey (find my recipient entry + decapsulate via window.pqDmOpen), aesGcmEncrypt/Decrypt (crypto.subtle AES-GCM), buildGroupMsgV1 (payload {epoch, nonce, ct}), parseGroupMsgPayload. chat-groups-p2p.js: createP2pGroup now auto-issues an initial epoch_key sealed to the creator (so chat works immediately for the creator); openP2pGroupDialog rewritten into a real chat view — fetches the latest epoch key + decrypts message history + renders, compose box (Enter to send) encrypts via AES-GCM and POSTs group_msg_v1, polls every 4s while open.
